segments.text: Behold, my eye has seen all this, My ear has heard and understood it.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But I would speak to the Almighty, And I desire to reason with God.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But you forgers of lies, You are all worthless physicians.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh, that you would be silent, And it would be your wisdom!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now hear my reasoning, And heed the pleadings of my lips.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will you speak wickedly for God, And talk deceitfully for Him?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will you show partiality for Him? Will you contend for God?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will it be well when He searches you out? Or can you mock Him as one mocks a man?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He will surely rebuke you If you secretly show partiality.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will not His excellence make you afraid, And the dread of Him fall upon you?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Your platitudes are proverbs of ashes, Your defenses are defenses of clay.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Hold your peace with me, and let me speak, Then let come on me what may!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do I take my flesh in my teeth, And put my life in my hands?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though He slay me, yet will I trust Him. Even so, I will defend my own ways before Him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He also shall be my salvation, For a hypocrite could not come before Him.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Listen carefully to my speech, And to my declaration with your ears.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: See now, I have prepared my case, I know that I shall be vindicated.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Who is he who will contend with me? If now I hold my tongue, I perish.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Only two things do not do to me, Then I will not hide myself from You:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Withdraw Your hand far from me, And let not the dread of You make me afraid.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then call, and I will answer; Or let me speak, then You respond to me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do You hide Your face, And regard me as Your enemy?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will You frighten a leaf driven to and fro? And will You pursue dry stubble?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For You write bitter things against me, And make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You put my feet in the stocks, And watch closely all my paths. You set a limit for the soles of my feet.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Man decays like a rotten thing, Like a garment that is moth-eaten.
